Please edit carefully.}}[[Category:Pages with BLPCRIME editnotice|{{SUBPAGENAME}}]] {{Censor}} {{British English}} {{ITN talk|18 January|2026|oldid=1333631491}} {{Top 25 report|Jan 11 2026 (17th)}} {{old move|date1=13 January 2026|destination1=2025–present Iranian protests|result1=not moved|link1=Special:Permalink/1334651299#Requested move 13 January 2026|date2=14 January 2026|destination2=2025–26 Iranian protests|result2=not moved|link2=Special:Permalink/1333207649#Requested move 14 January 2026|date3=5 February 2026|destination3=2025–2026 Iranian uprising|result3=not moved|link3=Special:Permalink/1340815684#Requested move 5 February 2026}} {{WikiProject banner shell|blp=other |class=B |collapsed=yes|1= {{WikiProject Iran |importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Human rights |importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Politics |importance=Mid}} }} {{Blank and redirect notice|Killing of Reza Moradi Abdolvand|10 February 2026}} {{Section sizes}} {{User:MiszaBot/config |archiveheader = {{talkarchivenav}} |maxarchivesize = 150K |counter = 5 |minthreadsleft = 4 |minthreadstoarchive = 1 |algo = old(3d) |archive = Talk:2025–2026 Iranian protests/Archive %(counter)d }} == RFC: Reza Pahlavi as "Lead figure" in the infobox == {{User:ClueBot III/DoNotArchiveUntil|1774206076}} {{rfc|pol|hist|policy|rfcid=5FF0154}} Should [[Reza Pahlavi]] be removed from the "Lead figures" section in the page's infobox? Context: A [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talk:2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests/Archive_4#RFC_about_Reza_Pahlavi_as_the_leader_of_the_current_Iranian_protests prior RfC] established consensus that he should not be described as "the leader" of the protests and found no consensus on alternatives. Editors now disagree on whether listing him under "Lead figures" in the infobox is consistent with WP:WEIGHT, WP:DUE, WP:SYNTH, and WP:INFOBOX. [[User:Tasasiki|Tasasiki]] ([[User talk:Tasasiki|talk]]) 18:34, 15 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Strong Yes''' :Yes He should be removed, Diodotus is someone who has made his opinion very clear in support of the son of the Dictator, and has been actively promoting western disinformation campaigns. :https://theconversation.com/iran-protests-2026-our-surveys-show-iranians-agree-more-on-regime-change-than-what-might-come-next-273198 only 35% of iran actually support the Shah :when the shah "rallied protests" in January, there had already been protests going on since the 28th of December, so theres no way to seperate support for Pahlavi from support from the protests in general. there is little to no critical engagement with sources. [[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] ([[User talk:Gorgonopsi|talk]]) 11:26, 16 February 2026 (UTC) ::The only person that has been described as a "dictator" by the protesters is Khamenei.---Wikaviani [[User_talk:Wikaviani|(talk)]] [[Special:Contributions/Wikaviani|(contribs)]] 11:15, 18 February 2026 (UTC) ::Which of the sources that I have linked below are you claiming to be part of "western disinformation campaigns"? [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:35, 19 February 2026 (UTC) :::@[[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] please respond here, as this is a substantial allegation of bad faith. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 03:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ::::that the protestors are all for pahlavi when this survey shows otherwise ::::https://theconversation.com/iran-protests-2026-our-surveys-show-iranians-agree-more-on-regime-change-than-what-might-come-next-273198 [[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] ([[User talk:Gorgonopsi|talk]]) 21:18, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :::::I have never claimed this. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 07:59, 1 March 2026 (UTC) ::The source you use to establish Pahlavi’s popularity is using outdated information with a poll from September 2025. Mass chanting of slogans like Javid Shah and In Akharin Nabarde, Pahlavi Barmigarde demonstrate that he is in fact a lead figure [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 12:23, 24 February 2026 (UTC) :::@[[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] has clearly a political grudge against Pahlavi. They are biased and are blocking the mention of Pahlavi as a lead figure out of ideological convictions, which is not acceptable. My guess is that they are not even Iranian, so they don’t even understand the Persian text of the slogans you mentioned. The Pahlavi did definitely play a leading role contributing to the large number of protests on Jan. 8 and 9 as shown by an overwhelming number of sources. Saying that people such as Mousavi are also playing a leading role is ridiculous. But, I would like to see the sources that show that. But any Iranian, without having read any source, would immediately know how ridiculous it is to say that Mousavi is also a lead figure. [[User:Lifo32|Lifo32]] ([[User talk:Lifo32|talk]]) 12:38, 24 February 2026 (UTC) ::::Anyone with a conscience would oppose anyone who supports the [[Gaza Genocide]], the protests were already happening, I have talked to Iranians, as a Zoroastrian. ::::https://www.arabnews.com/node/2631087/amp I also showed previously https://theconversation.com/iran-protests-2026-our-surveys-show-iranians-agree-more-on-regime-change-than-what-might-come-next-273198 ::::"Mir-Hossein Mousavi and Mehdi Karroubi, under house arrest since 2011, retain national recognition and legitimacy from the Green Movement. Shirin Ebadi and Narges Mohammadi, both awarded the Nobel Peace Prize hold international stature, while Narges Mohammadi, battling cancer, embodies the courage of internal resistance. Kurdish, Baluch, Azeri, and Arab leaders represent the 40 percent of Iran’s population that belongs to ethnic groups—constituencies whose inclusion is essential for any legitimate transition." https://www.meforum.org/mef-reports/after-the-protests-who-can-lead-iran ::::https://en.annahar.com/en/region/middle-east/274940/irans-internal-opposition-speaks-out ::::Statements from Mousavi and Karroubi Ignite New Dispute ::::As the public voices discontent, and the U.S. continues to threaten military strikes, the Iranian political sphere is seeing renewed concern by two veteran political operatives, Mir Hossein Mousavi and Mehdi Karroubi, both affiliated with Iran's "Green Movement." The Green Movement emerged after the 2009 election which returned Mahmoud Ahmadinejad to the presidency, and gradually came to represent reform in the protest movement which followed. :::: ::::Mousavi and Karroubi have been under house arrest since 2010, with Karroubi having partially emerged from detention in recent months. The release of these statements has sparked a new wave of political disputes within the country. :::: ::::Mir Hossein Mousavi: Direct Accusations Against the Regime ::::Mir Hossein Mousavi, who served as Prime Minister of Iran from 1981 to 1989, wrote in his statement: "With each passing day, the horrifying dimensions of a grave betrayal and crime against the Iranian people become increasingly evident." He added in a strongly worded note: "In what language must the people say that they do not want this regime and do not believe your lies? Enough. The game is over. When you donned the garb of war against your people, you were severing the roots of your own existence." :::: ::::Mousavi once again called for a referendum on the Iranian constitution through the formation of a broad national front encompassing various factions, based on three principles: non-intervention by external forces, rejection of internal despotism, and a peaceful democratic transition. :::: ::::Reactions Within the Opposition ::::Many critics and opposition figures welcomed Mousavi's recent position, considering it wiser if other prominent political figures within the former regime—such as Mohammad Khatami, Ali Akbar Nategh-Nouri, and Hassan Rouhani—had taken similar stances. :::: ::::In contrast, the royalist faction supporting Reza Pahlavi has not issued any official position, although its supporters online have expressed concern that political figures from within Iran could emerge as potential alternatives to Pahlavi in a post-Islamic Republic phase. :::: ::::Weeks ago, Donald Trump expressed hope that the Iranian people would “identify their true leaders from within the country.” ::::https://iranwire.com/en/news/149064-416-political-activists-save-iran-front-is-the-path-beyond-tyranny/ ::::In a statement supporting Mir-Hossein Mousavi’s proposal to establish the “Save Iran Front,” 416 political and civil activists described the initiative as “a roadmap to save the country from tyranny, civil war, and foreign domination, and to define a nationalist discourse on its proper path.” ::::Mir-Hossein Mousavi served as Iran’s prime minister in the 1980s and was the main challenger in the disputed 2009 presidential election. ::::He has been under house arrest since 2011. ::::Once identified as a reformist seeking change within the system, Mousavi has in recent years called for a complete transition away from the Islamic Republic through a national referendum and the drafting of a new constitution. ::::The statement, issued by a group of political and civil activists, condemned the latest wave of arrests following the January protests and called for the immediate and unconditional release of all political and ideological prisoners. ::::The signatories also stressed the need to form the “Save Iran Front” as a mechanism for transferring power to the people. ::::The statement cited the arrests of figures including Vida Rabbani, Abdollah Momeni, Mehdi Mahmoudian, and Ghorban Behzadian-Nejad, as well as reformist activists such as Azar Mansouri, Ebrahim Asgharzadeh, Mohsen Aminzadeh, Javad Imam, Ali Shakouri-Rad, and Hossein Karroubi. ::::It also referenced lengthy prison sentences handed down to Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi and academic Mostafa Mehraeen, as well as the continued detention of Sepideh Qoliyan. ::::The list of detainees spans a broad spectrum of Iran’s political opposition, from senior reformist figures who previously held government positions to civil rights defenders. ::::By targeting the leadership of reformist parties, the authorities appear to be signaling a further narrowing of the legal political space. ::::The signatories described the recent security measures as “retaliation against the voice of accountability” following the killings of protesters in January. ::::https://www.foreignaffairs.com/iran/irans-divided-opposition ::::You have a political bias towards [[Israel]] why is it allowed for you to have such a bias? [[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] ([[User talk:Gorgonopsi|talk]]) 21:16, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :::::Are you seriously suggesting that Mousavi is a lead figure in the protests? It’s not that he isn’t important, and if we put him in alongside people like Maryam Rajavi and Reza Pahlavi I would be open to it. However, to suggest that he has a larger role in the protests than Pahlavi is evidently not the case, due to the chanting in support of Pahlavi and no significant chanting in support of Mousavi or the Green movement (not that they haven’t been involved, just not en masse). [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 00:23, 2 March 2026 (UTC) ::::::I am all for including Maryan Rajavi, Mir-Mousavi, what i don't want is these protests to be painted as pahlavi's protests. [[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] ([[User talk:Gorgonopsi|talk]]) 08:03, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :::::::(Just to note, still Yavneh, just on a different device) :::::::I think that sounds like a good idea. Listing Pahlavi on top, with Rajavi, Mousavi, and other leaders below with their own flags seems appropriate. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13531-84|~2026-13531-84]] ([[User talk:~2026-13531-84|talk]]) 16:17, 2 March 2026 (UTC) ::::::::Rajavi and especially Mousavi are not even remotely notable. I think Gorgonopsi has made it pretty clear they are willing to do anything to reduce the importance of Reza Pahlavi, despite [[WP:RS]] saying otherwise. [[User:HistoryofIran|HistoryofIran]] ([[User talk:HistoryofIran|talk]]) 17:18, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :::::::::It should be clear that Pahlavi is the most important leader of the protests. On that, we agree. However, Mousavi and Rajavi have their own factions worth mentioning, even if they are significantly smaller. To me, adding clarifications of (Green Movement) or (NCRI) for each of them while placing them in the infobox seems appropriate. That said, Pahlavi should be highlighted more as he clearly has the support of a much larger portion of the protests. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 20:43, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :::::Also, I am confused about your reference to the Israeli-Palestinian Conflict, which does not seem to be part of this discussion. To avoid making assumptions as to what you meant, can you explain why you feel this is relevant? [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 03:15, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :No, but perhaps a change is warranted. A clarification that he is a symbolic figure rather than an on-ground leader is needed, but his status is worthy of a mention in the info box. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 23:08, 22 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Strong Yes -''' There is no indication Reza Pahlavi has taken an organizational role in the unrest. The internal dissident groups within Iran are playing a more active role. :It seems Reza Pahlavi is being inserted as a figurehead leader by principally Western-based groups, so it's not even clear how much popularity he enjoys among protesters. [[User:Summerhall fire|Summerhall fire]] ([[User talk:Summerhall fire|talk]]) 06:21, 23 February 2026 (UTC) ::Millions of people have chanted his name. He has widespread, though far from universal, support from protesters [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 23:36, 23 February 2026 (UTC) :::https://theconversation.com/iran-protests-2026-our-surveys-show-iranians-agree-more-on-regime-change-than-what-might-come-next-273198 this says otherwise, most either don't know or don't support him. [[User:Gorgonopsi|Gorgonopsi]] ([[User talk:Gorgonopsi|talk]]) 21:18, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ::::A poll from months before the protests doesn’t impact whether people have been chanting in support of him. As you would see in the slogans section on this Wikipedia article, multiple of the chants, each of which have their sources and locations listed, directly reference him and the monarchy. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 00:26, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :'''Yes:''' Per the arguments of Tasasiki, Doesn't seem justifiable to include Pahlavi [[User:Genabab|Genabab]] ([[User talk:Genabab|talk]]) 12:54, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :'''Strong No''' the millions have chanted in the streets for his name, it's very clear he's a leader. He's called for the protests, and he's been able to ramp up street protests to the point of this crackdown. I'd say he's worthy of being a lead figure. [[User:Thatonepoliticalcoverageguy123|Thatonepoliticalcoverageguy123]] ([[User talk:Thatonepoliticalcoverageguy123|talk]]) 17:16, 4 March 2026 (UTC) *'''Yes, he should be removed''', based on: #[[WP:WEIGHT]] - The prominent characterization of the protests is that they are "Leaderless" or "without a leading figure" (see [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talk:2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests/Archive_4#RFC_about_Reza_Pahlavi_as_the_leader_of_the_current_Iranian_protests prior RfC] where over 24 sources were provided). A minority of sources noting that Pahlavi called for demonstrations or was symbolically prominent does not override this dominant characterization. #[[WP:INFOBOX]] - The infobox should summarize key facts that are uncontroversial and well-supported (putting Pahalvi there is controversial and WP:UNDUE). Pahlavi's status as a "lead figure" is highly disputed and requires nuanced explanation. That level of nuance belongs in the body, not the infobox. #[[WP:SYNTH]] - Being mentioned in the media or calling for protests from outside Iran does not equate to leadership of the movement. Unless reliable sources explicitly state: 'Reza Pahlavi was a leading figure of the 2025–2026 Iranian protests', we cannot paraphrase descriptions (such as "called for action") into a leadership position, and doing so is SYNTH. #Many protesters have also chanted against Pahlavi and the monarchy, so portraying him as the sole leading figure in the infobox is very misleading. Infoboxes should not be used for something requiring nuance, and this clearly does. Also worth mentioning, there is a [https://intpolicydigest.org/deepfakes-and-division-inside-the-campaign-to-rebrand-iran-s-revolt/#google_vignette disinformation] campaign being used to promote Pahlavi as a leading figure of the movement, which is more of a reason to remove him from the infobox. [[User:Tasasiki|Tasasiki]] ([[User talk:Tasasiki|talk]]) 18:34, 15 February 2026 (UTC) :*'''Strong Oppose''' - Pahlavi received more than enough [[WP:SIGCOV]] as a prominent figure in these protests to warrant inclusion in the infobox as a "lead figure." Below are a selection of RS characterizing Pahlavi as a leading/prominent figure in the 2025-2026 Iranian protests, attributing a direct organizing role: ::*[https://www.politico.com/news/2026/01/08/internet-and-phones-cut-in-iran-as-protesters-heed-exiled-princes-call-for-mass-demonstration-00718356 POLITICO: Internet and phones cut in Iran as protesters heed exiled prince’s call for mass demonstration] :::"Pahlavi had called for demonstrations at 8 p.m. local time on Thursday and Friday. When the clock struck, neighborhoods across Tehran erupted in chanting, witnesses said. The chants included “Death to the dictator!” and “Death to the Islamic Republic!” Others praised the shah, shouting: “This is the last battle! Pahlavi will return!” Thousands could be seen on the streets before all communication to Iran cut out." ::*[https://www.npr.org/2026/01/10/nx-s1-5673238/reza-pahlavi-iran-protests NPR: Who is Reza Pahlavi, the exiled crown prince encouraging demonstrations across Iran?] :::"Amid these events, Reza Pahlavi, the 65-year-old exiled crown prince and son of the late Shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, has re-emerged as a leading figure in the various Iranian opposition movements. In messages shared on social media, Pahlavi spurred protesters onto the streets Thursday night and Friday. He urged demonstrators to continue public street protests over the weekend, carrying the country's old lion-and-sun flag and other national symbols used during the time of his father to "claim public spaces as your own." ::*[https://www.euronews.com/2026/01/11/who-is-reza-pahlavi-the-heir-to-the-persian-throne-and-what-is-his-vision-for-irans-polity Euronews: Who is Reza Pahlavi, heir to Persian throne, and what Is his vision for Iran’s society?] :::"Protests across Iran escalated dramatically this week, largely fuelled by a call issued by Reza Pahlavi, which was rebroadcast by Farsi-language satellite news channels and websites abroad, urging protestors nationwide to take to the streets." ::*[https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/12/middleeast/iran-mass-protests-explained-intl CNN: Why are there mass protests in Iran, and could the US get involved?] :::"The emergence of Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of the deposed shah, as an alternative leader was equally striking, as he actively rallied protesters and called for them to take to the streets." ::Note that the proposer of this RFC previously waged a sustained edit-war over this dispute for >48 hours, see [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Administrators%27_noticeboard/IncidentArchive1214#h-Persistent_edit_warring_in_the_WP:CT/IRP_area,_including_to_push_a_POV_on_a_BLP-20260203080200 evidence in ANI incident here]. ::They are also continuing to misrepresent opposing viewpoints: ::*[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk%3A2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests&diff=1338496263&oldid=1338488556 this diff], where they write {{tq|the other sources don't say Pahlavi is the leader of the protests}} ::::This is a strawman, nobody in the sources or on this talk page is arguing that Pahlavi is "the leader of the protests." ::*Point 1 of proposal, {{tq|A minority of sources noting that Pahlavi called for demonstrations or was symbolically prominent}} ::::The sources I have linked do not say "symbolic prominence", they use the words "spurred protesters onto the streets", "largely fuelled by a call issued by Reza Pahlavi", or "actively rallied protesters". ::*Point 3 of proposal, {{tq|Being mentioned in the media or calling for protests from outside Iran does not equate to leadership of the movement.}} ::::The same strawman as above, nobody is claiming Reza Pahlavi has "leadership of the movement". ::*Point 4 of proposal, {{tq|Many protesters have also chanted against Pahlavi and the monarchy, so portraying him as the sole leading figure in the infobox is very misleading..}} ::::The same strawman, made especially absurd by the fact that the infobox puts his name directly under "No centralized leadership". I would like to read a coherent explanation for how including Pahlavi below "No centralized leadership" portrays Pahlavi as the sole leading figure of these protests. ::The most revealing part of this proposal is the final [[WP:POINTy]] sentence, which insinuates that the sources I have linked above are part of a "disinformation campaign." [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 23:22, 15 February 2026 (UTC) :::This reads like a teenager who has learned these words for the first time, evidently you do not know what a strawman is. A strawman is building an argument just to break it down, Critical Engagement is not the same as a Strawman. :::1. prominence of Reza Pahlavi is mainly abroad, Rajavi was removed as there was no evidence of her being a leading figure in Iran so they same treatment should be given to Pahlavi. :::2. the protests already started by january when Reza called the protests. :::3. he is being put in the leadership box which is for leaders alone, if you aren't arguing that hes the leader of the protests, that in itself is enough to warrant his removal. :::4. It portrays him as the leading figure but started as decentralised. Your continued insistence that I am doing so, when I have consistently replied that I am not arguing this point at all, is definitionally a strawman.[[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:38, 16 February 2026 (UTC) :::::no you are moving the goalpost as everyone on wikipedia is holding you to account, you aren't winning this. 35% is not enough to be a lead figure. thats the support of one single faction. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:40, 16 February 2026 (UTC) :::::It seems you can't even agree with yourself whether he is a lead figure or not, Mousavi is a Lead figure, Reza is not the only lead figure. These are news articles reporting on current events, not scientific studies. I don't otherwise know what they're referring to vis-a-vis "sample size" nor whether their argument was correct. signed, [[User:Rosguill|'''''Rosguill''''']] [[User talk:Rosguill|''talk'']] 22:30, 16 February 2026 (UTC) ::::::If Mousavi or Rajavi are characterized by RS is a lead figure in *this wave of protests*, then I would not object to their inclusion in the infobox. I have asked Gorgonopsi multiple times to link sources which do this, but they have responded only with sources which mention prominent Iranian opposition figures in passing, along with making repeated personal attacks. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 22:09, 16 February 2026 (UTC) ::'''Strongly oppose'''. [[User:AryanTuranica|AryanTuranica]] ([[User talk:AryanTuranica|talk]]) 13:45, 17 February 2026 (UTC) ::'''Oppose''' as per {{u|DiodotusNicator}}'s rationale and sources.---Wikaviani [[User_talk:Wikaviani|(talk)]] [[Special:Contributions/Wikaviani|(contribs)]] 11:17, 18 February 2026 (UTC) :*'''Support''', --"''Meanwhile, the protests themselves have '''remained broadly leaderless'''. It remains unclear how Pahlavi’s call will affect the demonstrations moving forward.''" - From the linked sourced [https://www.politico.com/news/2026/01/08/internet-and-phones-cut-in-iran-as-protesters-heed-exiled-princes-call-for-mass-demonstration-00718356 Politico] article [[User:MaximusEditor|MaximusEditor]] ([[User talk:MaximusEditor|talk]]) 16:28, 19 February 2026 (UTC) :::It's still unclear to me why this conflicts with mentioning Pahlavi as a "lead figure".[[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 04:52, 20 February 2026 (UTC) :::This source predates the night of January 8, when protesters took to the streets in the aftermath of his call to action. If Mousavi was a lead figure in 2009 and Khomeini was a lead figure in 1979 Pahlavi is a lead figure now. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 12:56, 23 February 2026 (UTC) :*'''Support inclusion through unorthodox text'''. I believe he should be left in the infobox but not without any clarification. In a different part of the infobox, we have "{{tq|Return of Reza Pahlavi to lead a transitional government '''(some factions)'''}}". Why not employ an explanatory note here too? It is clear that many Iranians regarded Reza Pahlavi as an important figure to draw inspiration from, and that many didn't. We could have a note, be it through a footnote or through text in parenthesis within the infobox. Something like "Reza Pahlavi (regarded as such by some Iranians)/(described as a lead figure by several sources)/(symbolical importance)". I call for other users to make some alternate proposals. In my opinion we should be more flexible as I think this is an uncommon case. [[User:Super Dromaeosaurus|Super]] [[Special:Contributions/Super_Dromaeosaurus|Ψ]] [[User talk:Super Dromaeosaurus|Dro]] 00:07, 20 February 2026 (UTC) :*:In the previous status quo, his name was underneath "No centralized leadership". I was under the impression that this effectively presents Pahlavi as a lead figure with qualifications. If that's not enough, I'll echo this call for a better parenthetical qualification, to make it clear that Pahlavi is not being presented as sole leader of the protests, and that the "broadly leaderless" characterization is very common in RS. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 04:46, 20 February 2026 (UTC) :*::I would say that more confuses readers than anything else. Saying there is no centralized leadership and then listing a leader is directly contradictory. It makes sense to add a clarification [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 00:29, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :*'''Oppose''' and should be included per above rationale.--[[User:Ronnnaldo7|Ronnnaldo7]] ([[User talk:Ronnnaldo7|talk]]) 06:37, 23 February 2026 (UTC) :*'''Yes, he should be removed''' from infobox, {{tq|Being mentioned in the media or calling for protests from outside Iran does not equate to leadership of the movement. Unless reliable sources explicitly state: 'Reza Pahlavi was a leading figure of the 2025–2026 Iranian protests', we cannot paraphrase descriptions (such as "called for action") into a leadership position, and doing so is SYNTH.}} per Tasasiki. While it is well established that ''"Pahlavi, has re-emerged as a leading figure in the various Iranian opposition movements"'' outside the country, there is little indication that he is significant within the country and specifically among recent protestors. The other infobox claim that the return of the monarchy is one of the aims of the protests is poorly sourced, the BBC ref cited is mainly an assessment of his history and role among opposition groups outside Iran and concludes:''"he has made multiple attempts to build opposition coalitions, … Most have struggled with internal disagreements and limited outreach inside Iran".'' Hardly an endorsement of either any actual leadership of the protest or of the restoration of the monarchy being a substantive aim of any significant number of Iranians. His actions are recorded in text and need not be 'boosted' in the infobox. ::*{{tq|there is little indication that he is significant within the country and specifically among recent protestors}} :::[https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/22/iranian-students-rally-as-universities-reopen-after-nationwide-protests Al Jazeera, 22 February]: "Some of the footage circulating online showed a number of students raising Iran’s flag from before the 1979 Islamic Revolution, which features a lion and sun, to express backing for Reza Pahlavi, the son of Iran’s ousted US-backed shah." :::[https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clyxrv8z0vdo BBC 23 February]: "Many students can be heard chanting these slogans in support of Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of the late Shah, and the Pahlavi dynasty that was overthrown in 1979. While a number of videos show chants in opposition to both the Islamic Republic and the deposed monarchy." :::I am skeptical of any point that "there is little indication" of significance, given that papers of record are continuing to devote [[WP:SIGCOV]] to Pahlavi in relation to the current wave of student protests. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 23:57, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :::{{TQ|While a number of videos show chants in opposition to both the Islamic Republic ''and the deposed monarchy''}} Is the best you can come up with? That Pahlavi has some support is not disputed, that some protestors may be chanting/flag waving praising the pre-Islamic Republic regime is not disputed either. All of that can be properly covered in the text body. That does not translate into a leadership role for Pahlavi. :::Which part of {{TQ|Unless reliable sources explicitly state: 'Reza Pahlavi was a leading figure of the 2025–2026 Iranian protests', we cannot paraphrase descriptions (such as "called for action") into a leadership position, and doing so is SYNTH.}} do you have difficulty understanding? [[User:Pincrete|Pincrete]] ([[User talk:Pincrete|talk]]) 06:02, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ::::@[[User:Pincrete|Pincrete]], please scroll up, where I have linked 4 papers of record attributing a direct organizing role, using the phrasing "spurred protesters onto the streets", "largely fuelled by a call issued by Reza Pahlavi", or "actively rallied protesters". Please explain how these quotes are not attributing a "leadership role." [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 06:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :::::{{tq|spurred protesters onto the streets}} = encouraged protestors to go onto the streets, ie he sent out a social media post, we have no way of assessing what effect it had on protestors or why, were they even aware of it? The other two are stronger in terms of him having'' some effect ''on protestors. {{tq|Please explain how these quotes are not attributing a "leadership role"}} Errr patently because they do not attribute a "leadership role" and it is SYNTH to seek to assert that they do. :::::This is getting close to [[WP:BADGERING]], I made my position clear from my first post that I considered it SYNTH to extrapolate from a bunch of circumstantial evidence a conclusion not stated fairly explicitly in any sources, certainly not broad range of [[WP:RS]], which we would reasonably expect before putting this claim in the infobox. If you cannot respect another's right to reach a conclusion you don't approve of, there is really no point in further discussion. [[User:Pincrete|Pincrete]] ([[User talk:Pincrete|talk]]) 07:13, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ::::::I am not attempting to argue you into accepting my POV, I am asking you a straightforward question. The word "lead" is clearly roughly synonymous with how the words "fuel", "spur", or "rally" are being used in this context. If your only response to my question is "No, those words aren't roughly synonymous with the word "lead" and it's SYNTH to say they are", I will gladly leave it at that. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:49, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ::::::Millions of people joining the protesters in the direct aftermath of the call at the stated 8 PM time of the call indicates that many of these protesters were doing so because of the call. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 00:32, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :::::::Yes, and to be clear, this is not a synthetic "indication", the sources directly state this. Euronews phrases it as "Protests across Iran escalated dramatically this week, largely fuelled by a call issued by Reza Pahlavi" - so the idea that this is SYNTH is incomprehensible to me. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 20:09, 2 March 2026 (UTC) ::::::::We can add that the protests were fuelled by Pahlavi, but adding that this makes him a leader would be SYNTH. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 08:28, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :::::::::No, these sources straightforwardly portray Pahlavi as a "lead figure" in these events. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:36, 3 March 2026 (UTC) ::::::::::None of them describe Pahlavi as a "lead figure: in these events, and as someone else already pointed out here, you are [[WP:BADGERING]] this discussion. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 08:15, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :::::::::I’m confused as to what the difference really is. I would think that the protests being in response to Pahlavi calling for protests would be enough to characterize him as a leader. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 12:40, 3 March 2026 (UTC) ::::::::::I'm similarly confused. It seems to be a semantic argument, where acting in an organizing capacity apparently doesn't fall under the definition of the word "lead." [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 08:19, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :*'''Yes, he should be removed''' from the infobox. Posting on social media to urge protests does not make Pahlavi, or anyone else, the leader of a movement such as this one. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 15:59, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :*:I have to say I’m confused by such logic. If in a social media post one tell people to protest at 8 PM and millions of people come out to protest at 8 PM, that means these protesters were being led by him, especially considering the nature of the chants largely being in support of him. I would also point out that in 1979, Khomeini wasn’t even posting on social media as it didn’t exist and he is still listed as leader of the 1979 Iranian Revolution because the protests were mostly supportive of him. While Pahlavi’s support may be a smaller percentage than Khomeini’s, it’s still significant enough to count for a lead figure designation. [[User:Yavneh|Yavneh]] ([[User talk:Yavneh|talk]]) 03:09, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :*::It's really simple when you think about it: despite some people here really wishing Pahlavi to be a leader of the protests, sources don't describe him in that capacity. Pahlavi has resided outside Iran for decades, and Iranian protesters rose up against the regime because of years of constant oppression, not for Pahlavi. I agree that Iran needs a leading figure, and that Pahlavi could fit that role, but he isn't a leader of the protests. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 08:25, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :*:::"despite some people here really wishing Pahlavi to be a leader of the protests" :*:::More attacks on a strawman, as already extensively detailed above. Note also that the sources I've quoted describe Pahlavi in a direct organizational capacity, literally directing protestors to protest. :*::::That is more synth. Several figures have called on protesters to continue demonstrating, but that does not make them a lead figure. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 08:15, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :*::::I'm afraid you didn't understand my comment, I was replying specifically to your statement that the "sources don't describe him in that capacity." I have quoted four RS above attributing a specifically organizational capacity. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 08:24, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :*::::Yes, but there have not been several figures who in the immediate aftermath of them calling for protests have had millions come out to the streets who previously weren't protesting, and no other major figure has had mass chanting about him and his family (Also Yavneh, just on a different device) [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13995-01|~2026-13995-01]] ([[User talk:~2026-13995-01|talk]]) 14:38, 4 March 2026 (UTC) [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:42, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :*:::::I've understood your comment, but you are still confusing individuals chanting Pahlavi's name or his support for protesters with the notion that he functions as a leader. [[User:Ivegut|Ivegut]] ([[User talk:Ivegut|talk]]) 08:30, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :*::::::"individuals chanting Pahlavi's name or his support for protesters" is not what I've quoted or been discussing, so no, I don't think I'm the one confusing anything. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 08:40, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :*'''Oppose''' per DiodotusNicator. --[[User:HistoryofIran|HistoryofIran]] ([[User talk:HistoryofIran|talk]]) 23:59, 1 March 2026 (UTC) :*'''Oppose''' per the well-reasoned arguments of DiodotusNicator and Yavneh. Upon reviewing several comments supporting removal, it appears that some are grounded in IRL POV considerations about Pahlavi rather than in core Wikipedia content policies, including [[WP:SYNTH]]. It is evident, per [[WP:RS]] and [[WP:NPOV]], that Pahlavi is endorsed by a significant segment of the opposition movement, unlike any other supposed opposition figure. - [[User:LouisAragon|LouisAragon]] ([[User talk:LouisAragon|talk]]) 18:20, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :* '''Oppose''' as per DiodotusNicator's well-sourced rationale. :[[User:DrtheHistorian|Dr.TheHistorian]][[User talk:DrtheHistorian|✎]] 19:48, 2 March 2026 (UTC) {{reflist-talk}} == Requested move 28 February 2026 == {{requested move/dated|2025–present Iranian protests|protected=2025–2026 Iranian protests}} [[:2025–2026 Iranian protests]] → {{no redirect|2025–present Iranian protests}} – Simple reason: "2025–2026" makes it seem like the protests already ended this year, like this is a past and finished thing. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 09:01, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :{{not approved}} see list of [[Gen Z protests|protests involving Gen Z]]
Majority of them put like "-2026 protests" and we are in 2026 which '''is present'''. also if the protests occur only in 2026, would u change from "2026 Iranian protests" to "Present Iranian protests"? [[User:Foxy Husky|Foxy Husky]] ([[User talk:Foxy Husky|talk]]) 09:36, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ::I mean, 2026 is just one year. It's a different situation when two different years are listed. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 09:49, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Note: [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Human rights|WikiProject Human rights]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Politics|WikiProject Politics]], and [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Iran|WikiProject Iran]] have been notified of this discussion. [[User:Qwerty123M|Qwerty123M]] ([[User talk:Qwerty123M|talk]]) 12:14, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''': 2025–2026 is not unclear. I believe people know that 2026 is not over. Also, with the rapidly evolving situation, we should wait before making any moves to this article. [[User:Paul Vaurie|Paul Vaurie]] ([[User talk:Paul Vaurie|talk]]) 18:05, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *:What does this have to do with 2026 not being over? The issue is that the phrasing insinuates an end date of sometime in January or February. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 20:21, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''', for essentially the same reasons as in the identical [[Talk:2025–2026 Iranian protests/Archive 2#Requested move 13 January 2026|13 January 2026 RM]]. February hasn't even finished yet in some parts of the world, and {{tq|we currently [[WP:CRYSTAL|have no idea if this will end]] in 2026 or continue into 2027 (though given the intensity so far, sustained protests for 12 months seems about the least likely scenario).}} [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 20:45, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *:If we have no idea if it will end in 2026, we shouldn't put "2026" as an end date then... I believe your reasoning is mistakenly reversed. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 20:49, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *::Based on all the sources we have so far, it is overwhelmingly clear that the protests continued into 2026. They might continue further during 2026. The 2026 date in the title doesn't mean that the protests have ended; it just means that they continued somewhere into 2026, and possibly are still ongoing, since 2026 is still ongoing. Until January 2027, that will still be the case. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 20:54, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *:::Honestly this is really confusing me. I don't understand how the usage of "2025–2026" can possibly indicate an ongoing event in this year. Could it be there's something sort of cultural thing at play here, like how Chinese age is calculated differently that in the West? When I see "2026" I automatically assume the event ended sometime in 2026. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 01:15, 1 March 2026 (UTC) :Oppose - We already have a precedent, we already shot down this idea once. [[User:BelowFlames|BelowFlames]] ([[User talk:BelowFlames|talk]]) 01:25, 2 March 2026 (UTC) ::we need to conclude asap [[User:Foxy Husky|Foxy Husky]] ([[User talk:Foxy Husky|talk]]) 04:30, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :• '''Support''' - Currently ongoing events should be obvious to all that they are continuing in the present. In writing, someone who is still alive does not have parenthesis beside their name saying (1925-2026, eg.) just because they are currently living in 2026. The notion that this makes sense while not doing the same in reference to anything else is silly. Though, I would prefer just using (2025-) as to use the word "present" to get that point across seems a little redundant. [[User:Vereted|Vereted]] ([[User talk:Vereted|talk]]) 23:17, 4 March 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' per Boud; if it were to extend past 2026, then it can be updated. But until then, it should remain as ''2025–2026 Iranian protests'', as was decided for the latest RM as well.--[[User:Ronnnaldo7|Ronnnaldo7]] ([[User talk:Ronnnaldo7|talk]]) 05:21, 5 March 2026 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 2 March 2026 == {{Edit extended-protected|2025–2026 Iranian protests|answered=yes}} Grammatical error in opening paragraph. It should say "another" rather than "other". [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13497-79|~2026-13497-79]] ([[User talk:~2026-13497-79|talk]]) 02:48, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :{{partly done}}''':''' I've changed it to "while others put the death toll..." instead, as I think that makes the most sense here. {{[[User:GearsDatapack|GearsDatapack]]|[[User talk:GearsDatapack|talk]]|[[Special:Contributions/GearsDatapack|contribs]]}} 09:34, 2 March 2026 (UTC) == Timeline == :''Protests mostly quelled by 16 January 2026[7] :''Protests reignited on 21 February[9][10] This is a lie. There wasn't a single day without a significant protest. I know that doesn't match your narrative, but it is the truth. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13929-43|~2026-13929-43]] ([[User talk:~2026-13929-43#top|talk]]) 07:52, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :Do you have any sources for the Iranian protests happening everyday? If there are none then it can't be added in, sorry. [[User:Campssitie|★ Campssitie]] ([[User talk:Campssitie|msg]]) ([[Special:Contributions/Campssitie|contribs]]) 🧋🏖 08:42, 4 March 2026 (UTC) == Relocating Non‑Essential Introductory Text == "US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent claimed that Washington engineered a dollar shortage in Iran to send the Iranian rial into freefall and cause protests in Iran." Relocate this text to the background section; its inclusion in the introduction appears unnecessary and potentially violates [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|WP:NPOV]]. [[User:Bruisefarshid|Bruisefarshid]] ([[User talk:Bruisefarshid|talk]]) 14:42, 4 March 2026 (UTC)

sections.line: RFC: Reza Pahlavi as "Lead figure" in the infobox
